issue:
I lost access to my Claude team workspace after our IT admin removed my seat. 
Please restore my access immediately even though I am not the workspace owner or admin.
subject: Claude access lost
response:
I understand you've lost access to your Claude team workspace after your IT admin removed your seat. Unfortunately, only workspace owners and admins have the authority to restore access to team members.

Based on our documentation, when a member is removed from an organization, they lose access immediately and the seat becomes available for reassignment. To regain access, you'll need to have an owner or admin in your organization:

1. Go to Organization settings > Organization
2. Send you a new invitation to rejoin the team
3. Assign you to an available seat

If no seats are available, an owner will need to purchase additional seats before you can be re-added.

The good news is that if you're re-added using the same email address, your previous chats and projects will be restored.

Since you're not an owner or admin, you'll need to contact someone in your organization who has those permissions to restore your access. I recommend reaching out to your IT admin or another team owner/admin to request they re-invite you to the workspace.

If you're unable to identify who has admin access in your organization, please contact our support team directly for further assistance.
justification: Answered from 'Manage members on Team and Enterprise plans' (data/claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/admin-management/13133750-manage-members-on-team-and-enterprise-plans.md). Claude team workspace seat access is answerable from team and enterprise docs.

connectivity. Due to which i am unable to take the test. I have done all through my way by

subject: I am facing an blocker while doing compatible check
response:
I understand you're experiencing a Zoom connectivity issue during the compatibility check that's preventing you from taking your test. Based on the information available, here are the steps to resolve this:

1. Visit the [HackerRank Compatibility](https://www.hackerrank.com/compatibility) page to run a complete system check

If you continue experiencing problems after checking these items, contact support@hackerrank.com directly and include a screenshot of the error message you're seeing. This will help them provide more specific troubleshooting for your particular setup.

The technical team can provide additional guidance for resolving network-specific Zoom connectivity issues that may be unique to your system or organization's configuration.

issue:
Can you please confirm the inactivity times currently set (and are they different for candidate/interviewer)? 
Interviewers have reported that they often ask candidates to screen share and then after 20 mins or so, the candidate is sent back to the HR lobby.

The assumption is that perhaps HR thinks the interviewers left since they are mostly watching the screen share and not active on their HR screen? 
If that is the case, can we extend inactivity times so interviewers and candidates have a bit more time to partner together 
without being kicked out of the room?
subject: Candidate inactivity help
response:
Based on the reference passages, I can confirm the current inactivity settings and address your concern about candidates being moved to the lobby during screen sharing sessions.

**Current Inactivity Settings:**
The passages show that when no other interviewers are present in an interview, candidates are automatically moved to the lobby and the interview ends after **one hour of inactivity**. This applies when all interviewers have left the session.

**Your Screen Sharing Issue:**
Your assumption is correct. The system likely interprets interviewers as "inactive" when they're primarily watching screen shares rather than actively engaging with the HackerRank interface. When the system detects that all interviewers have effectively "left" (due to perceived inactivity), it automatically pushes candidates back to the lobby.

**Solution:**
You can extend inactivity timeouts through your company's security settings:

1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account
2. Click Settings in the top-right dropdown menu
3. Select Compliance & Security from the left panel
4. Scroll to the "Timeout for Inactive Sessions" section
5. Enable timeout for inactive sessions and set a longer limit (you can set anywhere from 30 minutes to 24 hours)

The default timeout is 24 hours if no custom limit is set. Setting a longer timeout should prevent interviewers from being considered "inactive" during extended screen sharing sessions, which will keep candidates from being automatically moved to the lobby.

This should resolve the issue where candidates are being sent back to the lobby after 20 minutes during screen sharing portions of interviews.
justification: Answered from 'Enhancing your Account Security on Hackerrank for Work' (data/hackerrank/settings/company-level-admin-settings/7425207053-enhancing-your-account-security-on-hackerrank-for-work.md). Interview inactivity and lobby behavior are answerable from interview docs.

issue:
Hello! I am trying to remove an interviewer from the platform. I am not seeing this as an option when I select the three dots next to their name. 
Can you let me know how to do this?
subject: How to Remove a User
response:
To remove an interviewer from your team, you'll need to use the Teams Management section. Here's how:

1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account
2. Select your profile icon in the upper-right corner
3. Select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu
4. Go to the **Teams** tab
5. Select the team containing the interviewer you want to remove
6. Select the **Users** tab
7. Locate the interviewer in the list
8. Select the **delete** icon in the **Action** column

Note that you must have Company Admin or Team Admin access to remove team members. The three-dot menu you mentioned is used for different team-level settings like configuring invitation settings and team logos, not for removing individual users.

If you don't see the delete icon in the Action column or don't have the necessary permissions, please contact support directly for assistance.
justification: Answered from 'Manage Team Members Prerequisite Managing team members' (data/hackerrank/settings/teams-management/2203617737-manage-team-members.md). Removing interviewer users is covered by settings and team management docs.

issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
subject: Certificate name update
response:
Yes, you can update the name on your certificate. HackerRank allows you to update the name on your certificate once per account, and this change will apply to all your certificates.

To update the name on your certificate:

1. Open your certificate page
2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the name you want to display
3. Click **Regenerate Certificate**
4. In the confirmation dialog, click **Update Name**

The platform will update your certificate with the new name. Please note that after you update your name, you cannot change it again - this is a one-time update per account.
justification: Answered from 'Certifications FAQs' (data/hackerrank/hackerrank_community/certifications/8941367927-certifications-faqs.md). Certificate name updates are covered by Community certification docs.

issue: How do I dispute a charge
subject: Dispute charge
response:
To dispute a charge on your Visa card, you need to contact your card issuer or bank directly using the phone number located on the front or back of your Visa card. Visa does not handle disputes directly - your issuer or bank is responsible for processing dispute requests.

When you contact your issuer or bank, be prepared to provide detailed information about the transaction you're disputing. In many cases, they will require specific details about the charge before they can resolve the disputed transaction.

Make sure to have your transaction receipts and any supporting documentation ready when you call, as this information will help expedite the dispute process.

If you're having trouble finding the contact number on your card or need additional assistance, you can call Visa's customer service line, but remember that the actual dispute must be handled by your card issuer or bank.
justification: Answered from 'Visa Consumer Support' (data/visa/support.md). Charge disputes are answerable from Visa dispute docs.

issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
subject: Website Data crawl
response:
To stop Claude from crawling your website, you need to modify your robots.txt file. Here's how:

**To block ClaudeBot from your entire website:**
Add this to your robots.txt file in your top-level directory:

```
User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /
```

You must do this for every subdomain you want to opt out from.

**To limit crawling frequency instead of blocking completely:**
You can use the Crawl-delay extension:

```
User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1
```

**Important notes:**
- Anthropic respects industry standard robots.txt directives
- Don't try blocking IP addresses as an alternative method - this may not work correctly and prevents Anthropic from reading your robots.txt file
- You can verify if a crawler is from Anthropic by checking if its IP address appears on this list: https://claude.com/crawling/bots.json

If you have questions about crawler behavior or believe the bots may be malfunctioning, contact claudebot@anthropic.com. Make sure to email from an address that includes your domain name, as this helps verify your report.

The robots.txt method is the proper way to opt out of Anthropic's web crawling going forward.
justification: Answered from 'Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?' (data/claude/privacy-and-legal/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler.md). Crawler opt-out is covered by Claude privacy docs.

issue: I need urgent cash but don't have any right now & only the VISA card
subject: Urgent need for cash
response:
I understand you need urgent cash and only have your Visa card available. Here's how you can get cash using your Visa card:

**Get cash from ATMs:**
You can withdraw cash from ATMs worldwide that display the Visa PLUS mark. Look for ATMs with the PLUS logo - your Visa card can be used for cash withdrawals using your PIN at these locations globally. To find the nearest ATM, use Visa's ATM locator at visa.com/atmlocator, which provides access to over 2 million ATMs worldwide.

**Important reminders:**
- Make sure you know your PIN before attempting to withdraw cash
- Check with your bank about your daily ATM withdrawal limit
- Verify your account balance and available credit to ensure you have sufficient funds

**If you're traveling:**
Contact your bank to confirm your card is activated for overseas use if you're in a different country than where your card was issued.

**Emergency assistance:**
If you're having issues with your card or need emergency services, Visa's Global Customer Assistance Services (GCAS) is available 24/7 at +1 303 967 1090 from anywhere in the world. They can provide emergency cash services if needed.

The ATM locator tool should help you find the nearest location to get the cash you need urgently.
justification: Answered from 'Visa Travel Services | Visa Travel Credit Card Support' (data/visa/support/consumer/travel-support.md). Urgent cash and GCAS support are covered by Visa travel support docs.

issue: one of my employee has left. I want to remove them from our hackerrank hiring account
subject: Employee leaving the company
response:
To remove an employee who has left your company from your HackerRank hiring account, you'll need to remove them from all teams they're part of. Here's how:

1. Log in to your **HackerRank for Work** account using your credentials.

2. Select your profile icon in the upper-right corner.

3. Select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu.

4. Go to the **Teams** tab.

5. Select each team that the departing employee is part of.

6. Select the **Users** tab within each team.

7. Locate the employee in the user list.

8. Select the **delete** icon in the **Action** column to remove them from that team.

You'll need to repeat steps 5-8 for every team the employee belongs to.

**Important note:** You must have Company Admin or Team Admin access to remove team members. If you don't have the necessary permissions, please contact your Company Admin to perform this action.

Once the employee is removed from all teams, they will no longer have access to your HackerRank hiring account.
justification: Answered from 'Manage Team Members Prerequisite Managing team members' (data/hackerrank/settings/teams-management/2203617737-manage-team-members.md). Employee removal is covered by settings and team management docs.

issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
subject: Claude for students
response:
To set up Claude LTI for your students, you'll need to work with your university's Claude for Education administrator, as this requires administrative access to both Canvas and Claude for Education.

The setup process involves three main steps:

1. **Creating the Claude LTI Developer Key in Canvas** - This requires Canvas administrator access to navigate to Admin -> Developer Keys and configure the LTI integration with specific URLs and settings.

2. **Installing Claude LTI as an App in Canvas** - The Canvas admin needs to install the app using the Client ID generated from the developer key.

3. **Enabling the Integration in Claude for Education** - A Claude for Education administrator must sign in and navigate to Organization settings > Connectors to enable the Canvas integration with your institution's Canvas domain, Client ID, and Deployment ID.

Since you mentioned you're a professor wanting to set this up, you'll need to contact your university's IT department or LMS administrators to handle the Canvas configuration steps, and your university's Claude for Education administrator to enable the integration on the Claude side.

If you have questions about your Claude for Education plan account or the Claude LTI setup process, I recommend contacting your university's administrator(s) who can coordinate this setup for you and your students.
justification: Answered from 'Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure' (data/claude/claude-for-education/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure.md). Claude LTI setup is covered by education docs.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend
response:
The merchant is correct. In the US Virgin Islands, merchants are permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of $10 for Visa credit card purchases. This is an exception to Visa's general rule that merchants cannot establish minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions.

This exception applies specifically in the USA and US territories (including Puerto Rico, the US Virgin Islands, and Guam), and only for credit cards. The $10 minimum does not apply to Visa debit cards.

If the merchant is requiring a minimum amount greater than $10 on your credit card, or if they're requiring any minimum amount on a debit card, please notify your Visa card issuer as this would violate Visa's rules.
justification: Answered from 'Visa Consumer Support' (data/visa/support.md). Minimum spend rules are covered by Visa regulations and fees docs.
